note: Majority of deaths from starvation and disease caused by federal blockade; direct combat deaths ~100,000; widely regarded as Africa's deadliest post-independence conflict

source: Human Rights Watch / Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie / scholarly accounts
note: Triggered by January 1966 coup; Igbo residents in Northern Nigeria massacred in September–October 1966 pogroms; 1–2 million fled east; direct cause of Biafra declaration
Figures include direct killings by Boko Haram/ISWAP and Nigerian security forces; over 2 million IDPs remain in northeast; children constitute major casualty category via suicide bombing use

Boko Haram overruns military base and town of Baga, Borno State; Nigerian military initially claimed 150 dead; satellite imagery and survivor accounts suggest 2,000+; worst single incident of the insurgency
MEND-era conflict; oil production cut by 25–30%; 2009 amnesty ended major hostilities; civilian death toll from security force operations disputed
Inter-communal violence following Kaduna State's adoption of Sharia in February 2000; Christians and Muslims killed in retaliatory cycles; 35,000–50,000 displaced across the North

Lagos State governor declared curfew; military opened fire on unarmed protesters at Lekki Toll Gate; Lagos Judicial Panel confirmed at least 12 killed; government denied civilian deaths; CCTV footage deleted

source: ACLED / Council on Foreign Relations Nigeria Security Tracker
note: Fulani herdsmen-farmer conflicts across Benue, Plateau, Kaduna, Niger, Zamfara states; climate-driven cattle corridor encroachment; classified as terrorism by some governors; government denies systematic nature

source: Human Rights Watch / International Crisis Group
note: After Buhari lost 2011 election to Jonathan, supporters rioted in northern states; corper (NYSC member) volunteers killed; 2011 violence was a warning of institutional fragility before 2015 transition

source: SBM Intelligence / ACLED / Council on Foreign Relations
note: Armed bandit groups in Zamfara, Katsina, Sokoto, Kebbi states; mass school kidnappings including 344 Kankara boys (2020); Nigerian Air Force airstrikes have killed civilians; scale rivals Boko Haram at peak
